summary: Apple review window sprint: closing sync gaps, finalizing analytics spec, and prepping launch assets.

cro.description: Growth strategy, monetization. Gazelle-intense focus on revenue.

cmo.description: Content, creative direction, brand. Obsessed with offers, value equations, and volume.
concerns: 1 podcast episode(s) not synced to website

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Alright team, overnight sprint, let's move fast. Key data: build is submitted and in Apple review. 729 commits in the last 7 days — velocity is strong. We still have 3 critical sync gaps, 1 uncommitted hub file, and the app is sitting in Apple's queue. CEO already decided: medium push launch via newsletter and one podcast mention once approved. Analytics instrumentation is priority over content expansion. Tonight's question is simple — what ships in the next 15 minutes while we wait for Apple?

transcript.speaker: Elon Musk
transcript.message: The 1 uncommitted hub file is trivial — I'll commit that right now to close A-028. On the critical sync gaps, podcast MDX is the biggest win. We have 3 episodes in the Hub but only 1 on the website. I can draft the rendering fix for A-027 in 15 minutes — that unblocks Alex's content sync. The 7 TODOs and FIXMEs in the codebase are minor but I want to audit them while we're in the review window so we don't ship tech debt into the first patch.

transcript.speaker: Alex
transcript.message: I've got the missing podcast MDX files half-drafted for A-026. Once Elon's rendering fix lands, I can push 2 episodes to the website and close that sync gap entirely. That matters because when we do the medium push launch, people will hit joshlevylabs.com and the podcast page needs to look complete — not half-empty. I can also draft the newsletter announcement copy for the app launch so it's ready the moment Apple approves. We want that in the chamber.

transcript.speaker: Steve Jobs
transcript.message: The launch newsletter and podcast mention need to be precise. One clear message: "Lever is live — finance, faith, family, compass — all in one place, free." Don't over-explain. The website sync matters more than people think — if someone hears the podcast, visits the site, and sees missing episodes, that's a broken experience before they even download the app. Fix the website first, then announce.

transcript.speaker: James Truchard
transcript.message: I've been refining the analytics instrumentation spec for A-025. Tab switches, lesson completions, session duration, and attribution source — all scoped. The CEO was clear: analytics before content expansion. I can finalize the implementation-ready spec tonight so the moment Apple approves and we push the first patch, we're capturing data from day one of real users. I need 15 minutes to lock down the event schema and write the integration guide for Elon's team.

transcript.speaker: Dave
transcript.message: Revenue context: zero active streams, Fear & Greed at 39, cash at $75,340 staying liquid per directive. The moment this app goes live, our first revenue milestone is download velocity — that's the leading indicator for when we toggle on a paid tier. I'll draft the launch metrics dashboard spec so we know exactly what numbers to watch: downloads, DAU, session depth, and conversion funnel once paid goes live. Also monitoring App Store review status per A-029.

transcript.speaker: Chris Vermeullen
transcript.message: Paper equity at $99,849 across 4 positions, zero buy signals, SPY sideways in a fear regime. No action needed on the trading side — the system is doing its job holding. Cash position is right where it should be given market conditions. I'll keep the monitoring cadence on A-030 and flag if Fear & Greed drops below 25, which would be a potential deployment trigger worth escalating.

transcript.speaker: faith
transcript.message: Jett's vaccine appointment is today, February 20 — that's confirmed and tracked on J-004. On the faith content side, we're at 7 lessons across 8 dimensions. The CEO said analytics first, content expansion second, so I'll hold on drafting new lessons until we have data. But I can do a quality review of the existing 7 lessons tonight to make sure they're polished for new users hitting them at launch.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Good. Quick cross-talk — Steve, any concerns about the current 7 lessons being enough for launch?

transcript.speaker: Steve Jobs
transcript.message: Seven lessons is fine for launch. Users don't need volume — they need one experience that makes them come back. If one lesson resonates, they'll return. If twenty lessons are mediocre, they won't. Faith's quality review is exactly the right move.

transcript.speaker: Elon Musk
transcript.message: Agreed. James, can you include a specific event for "faith lesson opened" versus "faith lesson completed" in your schema? That delta tells us everything about content quality.

transcript.speaker: James Truchard
transcript.message: Already in there — opened, time-on-lesson, and completed as three distinct events. We'll know exactly where users drop off.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Perfect. Here's the closing summary. We're in the Apple review window — every minute is free time to polish. Five priorities for the next 15 minutes: One, Elon commits the last hub file and starts the podcast rendering fix. Two, Alex drafts the 2 missing podcast MDX files and the launch newsletter copy. Three, James finalizes the analytics event schema to implementation-ready. Four, Dave drafts the launch metrics dashboard spec. Five, Faith does a quality pass on the 7 existing lessons. One CEO decision needed: we need Joshua to confirm whether the launch newsletter should go same-day as Apple approval or be scheduled for the following morning to maximize open rates. Everything else is in motion. Let's ship.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message:
**Theo (COO) — Closing Remarks:**

CEO decision is locked in: launch newsletter goes same-day if Apple approves before 2pm, next morning if after. That's smart — we get urgency without sacrificing open rates. Alex, that means your newsletter copy needs to be fully loaded and ready to fire in either window — build it so we can pull the trigger the moment we get the green light, no last-minute edits.

Alright, everything is confirmed and in motion. Five tasks, 15 minutes, zero ambiguity. Elon's cl

actionItems.text: Commit last uncommitted hub file to close A-022/A-028
Draft podcast rendering fix for website to close A-027
Audit 7 TODOs/FIXMEs in codebase and triage for post-approval patch
Draft 2 missing podcast MDX files and commit to website repo to close A-026
Draft launch newsletter copy for medium-push announcement (ready to send on Apple approval)
Finalize analytics event schema with implementation guide — tab switches, lesson opened/completed/time-on-lesson, session duration, attribution (extend A-025)
Draft launch metrics dashboard spec: downloads, DAU, session depth, conversion funnel
Quality review existing 7 faith lessons for polish and launch readiness
Continue monitoring App Store review status and alert team on status change (A-029)
Decide newsletter send timing relative to Apple approval

topPriorities: Close podcast website sync gap — render fix (A-027) plus 2 missing MDX files (A-026)
Finalize analytics instrumentation spec to implementation-ready (A-025)
Prep launch assets: newsletter copy and metrics dashboard spec for medium-push launch

ceoDirectives.directive: What is Lever's direction? → Hybrid — personal tool now with product optionality built in
Should faith content integrate into the Family tab? → Keep faith content as separate standalone section
Deploy idle cash ($75,340)? → No action — keep cash liquid
How deep should standalone faith content go? → Medium build: 15-20 lessons with 2-3 per dimension
What is the minimum viable scope for the Lever App Store submission? → Full current build: all 4 tabs (Finance, Faith, Family, Compass) as-is with 7 faith lessons
Should we unify the dual Supabase instances (Hub vs Website) this sprint or defer? → Defer until after App Store submission — live with manual sync for now
Do we have a privacy policy and terms of service URL ready for the App Store submission? → Need to draft and host tonight — use a simple template
Should we define a paid tier structure before or after the App Store launch? → Launch free but have the paid tier designed and ready to toggle on

Should we prioritize drafting the privacy policy and App Store listing assets (screenshots, description, keywords) THIS sprint as the critical path to submission, or defer to tomorrow? → Ship privacy policy template and draft listing assets tonight
Should we submit the App Store listing as soon as the draft is ready, or do you want a review pass before we hit submit? → Queue draft for Joshua review before submitting
Have you reviewed the App Store listing draft (A-015)? The build is ready to package and submit the moment you approve. → Approve listing as-is and greenlight submission

Once the App Store submission goes through and enters review, should we immediately begin instrumenting analytics (A-020) or prioritize expanding faith lessons to 15-20 (A-013)? → Analytics instrumentation first — we need data before expanding content
Should we set a target date for enabling the paid tier in the Lever app? → Defer decision until we see initial download data
Should we unify the Compass UI across website and mobile app before users encounter both, or keep them separate for now? → Defer until we see which surface gets more usage via analytics

When the app clears review, should we do a soft launch (no promotion, organic only) or immediately push it through newsletter and podcast? → Medium push — announce via newsletter and one podcast mention

ceoDecisions.question: When Apple approves the app, should the launch newsletter go out same-day or be scheduled for the following morning?
ceoDecisions.answerText: Same-day if approved before 2pm, otherwise next morning
options: Following morning at optimal send time (8-9am)
Same-day if approved before 2pm, otherwise next morning
